description:
Adds github and github-issues entries to .balls/config.json so balls itself dispatches both plugins on lifecycle events. Plugin configs go in .balls/plugins/{github,github-issues}.json pointing at mudbungie/balls. Local auth-dirs (.balls/local/plugins/<name>/) are per-clone and stay out of git.

This is balls dogfooding its own plugin protocol: bl lifecycle ops on this repo will fire the issues plugin to mirror to GitHub Issues; deferred-mode reviews would invoke the forge plugin (not exercised by the standard release-plz release flow, but configured for future use).

WHY in main rather than bare-repo-local: .balls/config.json is git-tracked on main; that's the operational config the project ships with. Other balls users running 'bl init' get a fresh config (no inherited plugins), so this doesn't impose anything on them — it's just balls's own self-config.

ACCEPTANCE: .balls/config.json plugins map includes github + github-issues; .balls/plugins/{github,github-issues}.json exist with correct repo/policy; binaries on PATH; an end-to-end manual test of the issues plugin push creates a real GH issue on mudbungie/balls.
title: Wire balls to its own GitHub plugins (forge + issues) — self-dogfood config
